user_message: I need you to process this exec meeting notes from Blue Yonder Airlines. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-12 12:06 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines whitespace analysis

Just checking in on the whitespace analysis proposal. Our CIO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 10:18 EST
Subject: Re: Re: whitespace analysis Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-26 13:40 EST
Subject: whitespace analysis - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with whitespace analysis. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Blue Yonder Airlines: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-E trap (Ignores ACR-during-dark signal); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-E failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-E failure mode (Ignores ACR-during-dark signal)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-E -- Ignores ACR-during-dark signal
